videos.title: DUBROVNIK, CROATIA | 10 BEST Things To Do In & Around Dubrovnik
videos.description:
Dubrovnik is located in the south of Croatia and is relatively isolated from the rest of the country due to a small nook of Bosnia & Herzegovina that intersects the land connection. For centuries, Dubrovnik was an independent republic thriving through extensive trade with other maritime ports. However, the city also suffered attacks, of which the early 90’s war was the most devastating one. Today, Dubrovnik is considered the most picturesque city on the Dalmatian coast thanks to its stunning architecture, beautiful scenery, and ancient walls that once protected the republic. In this video, we highlight 10 of the best things to do in and around Dubrovnik:

🔗 DUBROVNIK LINKS, ACTIVITIES, TOURS & MORE INFO

things to do in dubrovnik croatia
best things to do in dubrovnik croatia
videos.transcript: What's up guys, we're here in Dubrovnik, Croatia, and in this video we're going to show you the best things to do in and around the city. So hope you enjoy it! Dubrovnik is located in the south of Croatia and is relatively isolated from the rest of the country due to a small nook of Bosnia and Herzegovina that intersects the land connection. For centuries, Dubrovnik was an independent Republic, thriving through extensive trade with other maritime ports. However, the city also suffered attacks of which the early 90's war was the most devastating one. Today, Dubrovnik is considered the most picturesque city on the Dalmatian Coast thanks to its stunning architecture, beautiful scenery, and ancient walls that once protected the Republic. In this video, we highlight 10 of the best things to do in and around Dubrovnik, including an exciting day trip to its neighboring country. Make sure to watch until the end as we also reveal our top travel tips for your Dubrovnik trip. Now let's dive in! We're Zac and Ine. We make helpful videos about destinations around the world so you can easily plan your own adventures. The Old Town is the beating heart of Dubrovnik and generally the first and foremost place that you'll visit. It's listed as a UNESCO world heritage site and is full of historic landmarks, such as the beautiful City Walls and Rector's Palace. And while it's pleasant to just stroll around the marble paved squares and cobbled streets of the Old Town, we recommend going on a guided walking tour to understand Dubrovnik's history and culture on a deeper level. Find a link to the free walking tour that we did in the description below. The steep hill just behind the walled city of Dubrovnik offers stunning views over the old town as well as the neighboring islands. You can decide to walk or take the cable car to the summit. The cable car leaves near the Old Town, while the hike starts next to the highway and takes about two hours to get to the viewpoint and then back down to the bottom. At the top, there's a large white cross, fort, and a restaurant. But the best sunset views are observed from the open space to the west of the fort. Find a spot here and watch the sun set into the sea. For even better views of the Old Town, stick around because we'll get to that at number two. A fun, adventurous way to see Dubrovnik from the outside is by sea kayak. Taking in the city views from the water is something unique. But besides the Old Town views, it also provides the perfect opportunity to explore the rugged coastline of Dubrovnik. We kayaked to the most beautiful beach in the city called Cave Beach, which is only accessible via the sea. There are also some smaller hidden caves that you can kayak to or even the nearby Lokrum Island. You can rent your kayaks from the West Harbor or you can join a kayaking tour if you're not so experienced. Find more information about that via the link in the description. Walking the ancient City Walls was one of our favorite things to do in Dubrovnik. They are the defining feature of the city and offers some superb views over the Old Town and the shimmering Adriatic Sea. It takes about one to two hours to make the full circuit along the thick battlements, and you can start from one of its three ticketed entrances. The most fun aspects of the walk are the towers, forts, and hidden corners that you can find, as well as the glimpses it gives you into the courtyards. You can also see a beautiful island in the distance, which is another best thing to do in Dubrovnik that we can't wait to show you in a moment. We're on the top of the Old Town walls here and we just paid for tickets, it was about 30 euros each, which is pretty expensive. But it also includes the fort over there. And if you're here just for like a day or two, you might want to consider the Dubrovnik Pass because it includes a lot of the different stuff all around the old town in one price. So we'll leave a link for that in the description. Rector's Palace was the seat of the government when Dubrovnik was a self-governing Republic. It's a masterpiece of architecture and now houses the Cultural History Museum, where you can see how the local aristocats once lived. You can also find artifacts from the city's history here. We weren't allowed to take videos inside, but we do have some pictures so you know what to expect. Beyond the city lies a dramatic coastline that is a perfect place to cool off on a hot summer day. Don't expect sandy beaches, but rather rocky coves here. Banje Beach is the closest one to the city, just a five to ten minute walk from Dubrovnik's eastern gate. The beach is mostly pebbly with shallow and calm waters that are great for swimming. However, it gets crowded here during the summer so explore the beaches further away from the Old Town for a more secluded experience. Back inside the city walls of Dubrovnik, we recommend visiting some of the historic churches. In the Old Town, you can find many beautiful Gothic, Renaissance, and Baroque churches tucked in little alleys and squares. Our top picks include the Dubrovnik Cathedral, the church of Saint Blaise, and the Church of the Holy Annunciation. But our favorite one was the Church of Saint Ignatius, because of its beautiful frescoes on the main altar. If you want to leave the city behind for a day or you simply want to check off another country, then you should consider crossing the border to Bosnia and Herzegovina. Mostar is arguably the most beautiful town in the entire country, surrounded by picturesque mountains and a scenic river. The Old Town is known for its Ottoman-era houses and bridge. But besides that, Mostar also has an intriguing but shocking history, and more affordable food and drinks compared to Dubrovnik. Located around 130 kilometers or 81 miles north of Dubrovnik, this historic city can be reached by car or bus in a couple of hours. We went on a day trip here, which also included a stop at some very beautiful waterfalls. But it's the historic center of Mostar that is the main attraction to enjoy on this day. You can find a link for the tour that we did in the description below. So this is the best viewpoint in Mostar and actually it only cost you 1 euro to enter. It's right next to the mosque, so you can just like pay the fee and then just walk over. Alternatively, you you can do a day trip to the stunning Bay and City of Kotor in the neighboring country of Montenegro. We have an entire video about Kotor, including the best things to do in this fascinating place. Heading back to the city of Dubrovnik, Fort Lovrijenac is one of the most iconic sites that you'll find. It's worth paying a visit inside for two reasons: First and foremost, lots of scenes from the TV series Game of Thrones were filmed here. In fact, many places in Dubrovnik were featured in the series and you can visit them during a Game of Thrones walking tour, which we've included a link to in the description. Secondly, the fort offers an incredible view of the Old Town. But for the best view of the Old Town in our opinion, you should head to the hillside above Banje Beach, and we've put the viewpoint where we walk to on a map here. You can also find the exact coordinates in our blog post. Finally, you can't leave Dubrovnik without visiting nearby Lokrum Island. This island is viewable from the city walls that we mentioned earlier, but it's much more enjoyable to actually visit the island itself than simply see it from a distance. A short ferry ride connects the Dubrovnik Harbor to the island throughout the day. It's a swift way to escape the crowds and surround yourself with nature. The island is almost entirely covered with green pine forest. There are walking routes as well, which take you by rocky coves, an old monastery, a botanical garden, remnants of a fort, and a salt lake. You can easily spend a few hours to a full day here. As we promised at the beginning of our video, we'll now highlight a few tips for traveling to Dubrovnik so you can have the best trip possible. First, visit in the shoulder season if you can. The summer is hot and gets extremely crowded. We visited at the end of October and could not have asked for a better time. Second, take decent walking shoes with you and be prepared to walk a lot. The Old Town is a traffic-free zone so you have to walk around. No driving or cycling. Besides that, a large part of the Old Town streets are steep steps. The same is true for the neighborhoods surrounding the Old Town. Finally, stay outside the Old Town for cheaper accommodation deals and then take the bus or walk to the Old Town, as there's only limited expensive parking available. If you're planning your Croatia trip, we recommend keeping things organized with our interactive travel planner. Find a discount code below. We also included important links that you'll need for your Dubrovnik trip in the description, such as a full travel guide with where to stay and eat. We'll catch you on the next adventure!

safetyNotes: Don't expect sandy beaches, but rather rocky coves here

videos.description:
✅ Here’s the exact list I share with friends visiting Dubrovnik (my free guide): https://bit.ly/Croatia-Guide-Camden

These 24 tips to know before visiting Dubrovnik are what I wish I knew before my trip to Dubrovnik, Croatia. Hopefully these travel tips will help you from making some of my mistakes when traveling through Dubrovnik and the rest of Croatia!

The eSIM I use to keep my phone connected when in Croatia -- https://airalo.pxf.io/CamdenDavid --You can get 15% off by using code: CAMDEN15

Game Of Thrones locations (Credit to Greta's Travels - NOT AFFILIATED) -- https://gretastravels.com/game-of-thrones-filming-set-in-dubrovnik/

I'm part of an affiliate network and I receive compensation from partnering websites. This video is accurate as of the posting date but may not be accurate in the future.

direction of Oldtown in these areas right here these are going to be a little bit pricier they're going to be more Resort style but they're going to provide some great options we actually chose one of these as our wedding hotel and it was great for most of our guests so keep that in mind now in terms of getting around the city if you're in Oldtown itself you're really going to have one option and that is going to be walking but it is a great little town to wander through lots of cute little alleys and just beautiful scenery so you're really going to enjoy that definitely walking is the Uber but also keep in mind that bolt is something that is kind of like the European version of uber it's typically about 20% cheaper the last time I was in brnik Bolt was not operating there yet but I that's because there really two main gates and there's just one main road that gets really backed up especially around the busy times I've always found that if you can just walk about 5 minutes outside of that Oldtown area if you can walk to one of these little areas your ride is going to be able to get to you a heck of a lot quicker and it might actually be a cheaper ride so do yourself a trap cuz there is one place that seems to get so many people and that is Straton it is the main street right once you enter the old town it's a beautiful Street and you'll see a lot of different restaurants there but do not eat anything on this little strip it is all going to be a tourist trap overpriced not good quality get the heck away from there as much as you can also keep in mind that that I think you can realistically skip so the one that you've probably heard of is the walls and the walls you get to walk all around Oldtown and it is gorgeous it is stunning it is something that you're going to want to do you do have to buy tickets you don't need to worry about buying tickets too far in advance for it no worries there but do note that a lot of tourists end up making the mistake of doing that midday it is not a quick walk while Oldtown seems small it does take a while to get around like 45 minutes to an hour so do those as early in the day or as late in the day as possible for two reasons one I think it's most beautiful that time of day but two it's not going to be as hot and you're going to be able to avoid most of the tourist and also recognize that you're tickets for the walls it doesn't just grant you access to the walls there's also the main Fort that is right across the street I see a lot of tourists make the mistake of just skipping that Fort important things to note with the booa bar so the first is that they are cash only so make sure that you have cash on you second is that they do not have any food this is a drink only place so be prepared for that third is that it is first come first sered There Are No Reservations so get there as early as possible that way you can secure spots cuz a lot of people once they get a spot they don't want to give it up another of my personal favorite recommendations is a place called Beach Bard dodo I like it for a couple reasons the first being that it does actually have a little bit of food the second reason is it doesn't get as many tourists as the boo bars do still has gorgeous views overlooking a beach which is my next recommendation which is Beach shoit it is this little beach that again a lot of locals will go to it will fill up in the Summers but a lot of times you're not going to have a ton of people there it is small it is again a rocky Beach so be prepared for that but it's a really neat place to check out but I want to tell you about the biggest mistake that I think a lot of tourists make and by far the biggest one is thinking that Croatia is just the dalmatian coast and that you want to stay up on the water and that couldn't be further from the truth there's so many incredible places to see Inland that I definitely think are worth your time and checking out so you can learn more about them right here I hope this all was helpful and we'll catch you guys next time

videos.transcript: Dubrovnik, The world's most famous walled city in this travel guide we'll cover money saving tips, how to beat the crowds, and how to experience Dubrovnik like a local. So sit back hit that like button and we'll share everything you need to know when planning a trip to the pearl of the Adriatic, Dubrovnik When it comes to getting to Dubrovnik there are a few different travel options available one popular choice is to fly into Dubrovnik airport, which is located about 15 miles outside of the city center from the airport you can take a taxi shuttle bus or rental car to reach the old town easily given the most famous sites in the Old town are inaccessible by car we'd avoid renting a car unless you absolutely have to the main reason for this is that there are no cars allowed in the old city so you won't find parking near anything within the walls another popular option to get around is to take a bus or train to Dubrovnik from other cities in Croatia or neighboring countries this is what we did as we took an easy bus ride from the nearby city of Mostar to the Dubrovnik bus station the bus station is located right next to the cruise port so if you're making a more scenic arrival by sea getting to the old town will be the same as the bus there will be plenty of taxis lined up but since Uber is available in Croatia that's what we used to travel from the bus station to our Airbnb for us it was just under $12 for Uber to the old town so use this as a reference for how much a taxi should cost Uber is widely available around the Dubrovnik area and definitely is your friend if you're looking to get outside the walls we took an Uber to the city's Sky bike which was an amazing time it was nearby other viewpoints that offer some of the best views of the city and the Adriatic the cable car can also take you to the top but we found the price of an Uber ride was much more affordable than roundtrip Cable Car tickets and then we were able to hike downhill on to Dubrovniks most famous hike so consider having Uber downloaded if you're looking to try out some exciting spots beyond the walls while the Adriatic area is beautiful all year round the best time to visit Dubrovnik is late spring or early fall during these months the weather is mild and the crowds are much smaller than during the peak summer season this allows visitors to enjoy all that Dubrovnik has to offer from exploring the Charming Old Town to it relaxing along the beautiful beaches if you opt to travel during the popular summer season you can expect larger crowds in a much more intense heat and while you can expect highs to hover around the high 80s during these dog days of summer the number of rainy days in the summer is far lower than the rest of the Year this means you can beat the heat swimming at some of the most unique swim spots that Debrovnik is known for we cover some of Dubrovniks best swimming holes away from the tourist in our top things to do in Dubrovnik video as well as many other top attractions and sites in this incredible City so make sure you subscribed and check out that other video as well anyways while summer is the high season for tourists winter is the low season this can be a great time to travel like a local but you do run the risk of some cold gray and rainy days so keep this in mind when it comes time to book your accommodations we recommend staying just outside the city walls where lodging is cheaper and you won't need to Lug your luggage as far as if you were staying in the old town given there are no cars within the city walls if you're traveling with luggage it can be exhausting hauling your baggage up and down steps and across cobblestone streets there was actually a rumor going around that Dubrovnik was going to outlaw the use of rolling luggage inside the city walls and that travelers would be fined if their luggage wheels touched the ground but we haven't seen any credible news stations actually confirming this so for the time being it's safe to travel with rolling luggage we stay at this amazing Airbnb called the Eagle Nest and it was only a 10-minute walk down downhill to the city walls one major advantage to staying above the city is that we were able to experience some unbelievable sunsets from our private balcony and it was much easier getting our luggage from the taxi to our stay all right so for those interested in where we're staying in Dubrovnik this is our Airbnb and we'll give you a quick little tour here so let's start out in the bathroom here it's a pretty standard size has some modern updates which is great we have a nice rainfall shower pretty big sink pink toilet paper which I've never seen before so moving on this is the bedroom pretty comfy bed pretty big we have a nice big TV here which is actually a smart TV we have a hot plate here fridge that is disguised plenty of cooking utensils and something we're really excited about a dishwasher which is currently running so you might hear it in the background here moving on to the main attraction and the reason why we booked this Airbnb is the the gorgeous view of Dubrovnik so as you can see it's about sunset here and we're getting such a pretty view of the city it has been an amazing spot to sip your coffee in the morning and sip some wine at night so we highly recommend this spot I think it's a perfect location for seeing dubnick and it's actually pretty easy to get to too so 10 out of 10 would recommend as of 2023 Croatia has officially adopted the euro meaning this colorful money is the currency you'll use in Dubrovnik from our experience credit card was accepted everywhere we travel throughout Dubrovnik and we seldom had to use cash but it was nice having cash available if you need it if you're looking to stock up on cash there are plenty of ATMs called Bank mats around the old town with several lining Dubrovniks most famous street the Stradon so this can be a great spot to withdraw what you need if you're traveling to Dubrovnik chances are you want to visit the city's number one attraction walking the city walls this is one of our favorite things we did in Dubrovnik but unfortunately is one of the most expensive activities you can do here but there is a tip when it comes to purchasing City walls tickets that can save you money the cost of a single ticket to enter dubrovnik walls cost the same as a single day Dubrovnik pass and given the Dubrovnik pass includes the same city walls ticket purchasing the pass instead of individual tickets is a great way to ensure you get the most bang for your buck in Dubrovnik the pass also allows you to enter museums like the maritime History Museum which is housed in a beautiful historic building near Dubrovnik old port and is full of beautiful scale models of ships it also allows you to enter Rector's Palace the original house built for the ruler of old Dubrovnik complete with its own museum and the original dungeons which you can explore we enjoyed wandering these museums and even if you aren't a museum fan just knowing that you can pop into these museums for a quick bathroom break with the Dubrovnik pass alone makes this worth having you can either purchase your dubrovnik pass online or if you're like us you can purchase them from the ticket information booth right underneath the main clock tower on the East End of the city when it comes time to walk the walls it's also important to know that your ticket is only good for a single time entering the walls meaning you can't just pop up and down and in and out of the walls all day because of this you're going to want to be strategic about when you actually enter in peak traveler season the walls open at 8:00 a.m. and if you're an early bird this is the best way to have the walls all to yourself and to beat the heat by midm morning there will be tons of people on the walls and if cruise ships are in town you can expect a large influx of wall walkers during the time that they're in port so another great option is waiting until evening to enter given it takes between 1 and 2 hours to walk the full perimeter entering the walls about an hour before close is a great way to experience fewer crowds and walk the walls without Dubrovnik oppressive heat our experience was that while the ticket checkers are very strict about not allowing anyone to enter the walls after they're officially closed they don't actually kick people off the walls after hours meaning you can slowly continue to walk the walls even after it's closed and the crowds begin to thin out given there are several bars along the way you can even stop at the top and enjoy a drink and wait for the sunset to continue your journey which sounds fun as well and rest assured there are a few bathrooms inside the walls so if nature calls while you're up there you won't have to leave the walls to find a bathroom also included with your city walls ticket is access to The Fortress of St Lawrence this fort is located about 10 minutes outside of the city's famous pile gate which can be another top spot to see if you have a one day Dubrovnik Pass and plan to walk the walls in the evening you'll probably want to visit The Fortress in the morning since your ticket is only valid for one day however if you're instead purchasing a standalone City walls ticket you can walk the walls then the Fortress or vice versa within the 72 hours of your ticket being scanned which might give you more flexibility with Dubrovniks historical sweltering Summers finding ways to stay hydrated are a must in this city let's get to Michael inside the dubrovnik market area to share a quick tip on beating the heat one important thing to know about dubrovnik is the water from the city's water fountains is actually portable and deliciously drinkable it's cold fresh and on a hot day in Dubrovnik especially with dubrovnik being known for its punishing heat it's good know you can fill your bottle up all over the city so definitely bring a bottle when you visit Dubrovnik cheers while there are several spots to fill up your water bottle around the city the found near the pile gate is probably the most iconic being built nearly 600 years ago this Scenic Fountain has been hosting Croatian water cooler talk for centuries I mean if these Stones could talk am I right? Anyways this is a Scenic spot to fill up your bottle and is located next to both the Dubrovnik City walls entrance as well as The Franciscan Monastery which we talk about in our top things to do video so this is a great spot to sit and Sip and chances are with all that free water at some point in your stay you're going to have to use the bathroom there are several public bathrooms around the city most costing around 50 euro cents to enter as we mentioned before we were happy to have the dubrovnik pass as we could pop in and out of the museums and attractions on the ticket and use the restroom as needed without having to dish out any extra cash and one last thing on water if you've seen our top things to do in Dubrovnik video you'll know there are some semi-hidden swim spots just outside the walls which can be a great spot to beat the Croatian heat so consider bringing your swimsuit and towel if you're looking to take a swim in this one-of aind city so that's it for our Dubrovnik travel guide guys be sure to check out our top thanks to do in Dubrovnik video If if you're interested in the best spots to see in and around this town as well as some of the craziest attractions this city has to offer we also just released our Dubrovnik food tour documenting the dishes and restaurants we recommend and how much everything costs please be sure to give this video a like if you found it useful and subscribe to see our videos from all around the world we've got videos lined up from all over Greece, Kotor Montenegro and many spots in Asia so be sure you're subscribed so you don't miss it thanks and we'll see you in the next one bye [Music]

videos.title: DUBROVNIK, CROATIA - 20 Top Things to Do - 4K Dubrovnik Travel Guide
videos.description:
Dubrovnik is full of history, culture, delicious ice cream and stunning turquoise beaches. We just had to pay it a visit! We visited Dubrovnik, Croatia, in July for 7 days and were blown away by its beauty by its architecture and coastal scenery. In this guide, we cover the best Dubrovnik attractions in the old town, plus some excellent day-trips and things to do nearby.

From the famous Dubrovnik walls and the cobbled streets of the old town, to boat trips to the magnificent Elafiti islands and Lokrum island, there's plenty of inspiration in our Dubrovnik 4k travel guide.

We hope you find this guide informative and can use it to plan an upcoming trip. If you liked the video, be sure to subscribe to our channel and give it a thumbs up! 

🛏️ Where we stayed:
We stayed in the popular tourist town of Cavtat. It’s in a great location, too far away from Dubrovnik city centre. We stayed at Hotel Croatia and would highly recommend it - https://expedia.com/affiliates/konavle-hotels-hotel-croatia.331TDkT

Other hotel recommendations: 
😴 Dubrovnik Palace: https://expedia.com/affiliates/dubrovnik-hotels-dubrovnik-palace.pGEymtG
😴 Hotel Uvala: https://expedia.com/affiliates/dubrovnik-hotels-hotel-uvala.htQ6KgB
😴 Hotel Vis: https://expedia.com/affiliates/dubrovnik-hotels-hotel-vis.C5nblHa
 
💻 Follow us on socials: 
https://www.instagram.com/toucan_travellers/
https://www.facebook.com/toucantravellers/
Pin our posts on Pinterest - https://uk.pinterest.com/toucantravellers/

🔔 Subscribe to our channel: / ‪  @ToucanTravellers 
 
🔔 Subscribe to our secondary channel for hotel guides and reviews:  / ‪  @ToucanTravellersHotels

videos.title: Top 10 Things to Do in Dubrovnik, Croatia (2026 Guide)
videos.description:
✈️ Top 10 Things To Do in Dubrovnik, Croatia 🇭🇷
Planning a trip to Dubrovnik? This video covers the 10 best things to do in Dubrovnik, Croatia — from the famous Old City Walls to hidden gems, day trips, beaches, and unforgettable sea adventures.

🎯 Whether it’s your first time or your fifth, this is the perfect travel guide to help you build your Dubrovnik itinerary.

✅ What’s Inside:
#10: Walk the Old City Walls

#9: Buža Bar & Cliff Jumping

#8: Mount Srđ Cable Car

#7: Secret Alleyways of the Old Town

#6: Banje Beach

#5: Day Trip to Kotor (Montenegro) or Mostar (Bosnia)

#4: Game of Thrones Tour (for fans)

#3: Old Town Walking Tour

#2: Trsteno Arboretum (Hidden Gem)

#1: Sea Kayaking & Snorkeling Tour

👍 Like, comment & subscribe for more travel guides!
📍 Been to Dubrovnik? Let us know your favorite spot in the comments!
tags: Best places to visit in Dubrovnik
videos.transcript: Are you planning a trip to Drovnik, Croatia? Whether it's your first visit or your fifth, here are 10 unforgettable things to do in and around the city. From medieval walls to hidden beaches, and trust me, number two is a spot almost nobody knows about. So, without further ado, let's dive right into number 10. Now, if there's one thing that makes Dubrnik iconic, it's the old city walls. They wrap all the way around the town and give you insane views of the red rooftops, the Adriatic Sea, and the tiny alleyways below. Go early in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the heat and crowds. Take your time, stop for photos, and maybe grab a gelato before you start. Walking the walls is the best way to understand why this city is called the Pearl of the Adriatic. Now, at number nine, we have Bua Bar and cliff jumping. Tucked into the side of the city walls, you'll find a hidden entrance that leads to the Buzza Bar perched right on the cliffs. It's the perfect place to grab a cold drink and take in the sea views. But here's the kicker. If you're feeling adventurous, you can jump straight off the rocks into the Adriatic Sea. The water is crystal clear, the vibe is laid-back, and it feels like you found your own private escape. Now at number eight, ride the cable car to Mount Surd for the absolute best view of Dubravnik. Hop on the cable car up to Mount Surd. From the top, you'll see the entire old city laid out beneath you. The island scattered across the water and the mountains rolling in to the distance. Go around the sunset for the full magic. Bring a camera, maybe a snack, and just soak in the view as the city glows below you. Now at number seven, explore the secret alleyways. Most people stick to the main street, but the real magic of Dubnik is in the tiny back alleys. Wander without a plan, and you'll find hidden chapels, little cafes, laundry hanging from balconies, and locals going about their day. It's quiet, authentic, and honestly, one of the best ways to see the true character of the city. Number six, Ba Beach. Just outside the old town walls is Ba Beach, the most famous stretch of sand in Dubravnik. It's lively, easy to reach, and comes with incredible views of the old town and Locker Island right in front of you. The water here is clear and calm, perfect for swimming. And there are plenty of sunbeds, beach bars, and restaurants lining the shore. Sure, it can get crowded, but that's part of the fun. The atmosphere is buzzing and you're just steps away from the city's historic center. If you want a classic Dubrovnik beach experience with a postcard view, Bonja Beach is the spot. Next up at number five, we have a day trip to Qatar or Mostar. If you've got a free day and want to explore beyond Croatia, you've got two amazing options. Just a short drive from Dubravnik. Head south into Montenegro and you'll find the Bay of Qatar, one of the most stunning bays in the world. The mountains rise straight up from the water. The little villages feel frozen in time. And the old town of Qar itself is full of winding streets and hidden squares. It's like stepping into a fairy tale. Or you can head inland to Bosnia for a visit to Mostar. The city is known for its beautiful old bridge and riverside views. You can walk through the market streets, stop for a coffee, and see locals diving into the river. It's simple, charming, and a very different experience compared to Dub Bronik. Perfect for a day trip. Both options are doable in a day, and they give you a chance to see a whole different place while still basing yourself in Dubnik. Next up, at number four, we have a Game of Thrones tour. Now, I'll be honest, this one is a bit hit or miss. Many visitors joined the Game of Thrones tours since Dubnik was a major filming location. If you're a fan, it can be fun to see where some of the scenes were shot while exploring the city. But if you're not into the show, you may find the regular walking tours more worthwhile. Still, it's an option that adds a different twist to experiencing Dubnik. Next up, number three. And at number three, we have the Dubnik Discovery Oldtown Walking Tour. If you enjoy history, a walking tour of the old town is a great way to see Dubravnik on your own. It's easy to admire the streets and buildings, but with a guide, you'll hear stories that bring the city to life. They'll also point out small details that you might miss, like carvings and hidden corners. It's a nice way to get to know the city better. Now, at number two, we have the hidden gem. A historic seaside garden just outside Dubnik. Just 30 minutes outside Dub Brumnik is one of the oldest gardens in Europe and most visitors skip it completely. It's full of fountains, exotic plants, and stone arches overlooking the Adriatic Sea. If you're a Game of Thrones fan, you'll recognize it as the Garden of King's Landing. But even if you've never seen the show, it's peaceful, beautiful, and a refreshing break from the crowds of the old town. And finally, one of the most unforgettable things you can do in Dubravnik, see kayaking and snorkeling on the Adriatic. Starting right outside the old town, you'll paddle along the massive city walls with history rising behind you in endless blue water in front. Most tours take you out to Locker Island, where you'll discover hidden caves, rugged cliffs, and coes you can only reach from the water. Halfway through, you'll stop at the quiet beach for a swim and some snorkeling. The water is so clear, you'll see colorful fish and rocky reefs just beneath you. Seeing Dubravik from the sea gives you a completely new perspective, and it's one of those adventures that just sticks with you long after your trip is over. Dubravnik is more than just a pretty old town. It's a city of hidden gems, seaside adventures, and timeless history. If you've been here before, let us know your favorite spot in the comments. And if you're planning your first trip, I hope this list gave you some fresh ideas. Thanks for watching and we'll see you in the next Barjacks travel adventure.

safetyNotes: Go early in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the heat and crowds
